object.setup_prose: You are assisting a Harvard residential life coordinator who is evaluating the response times of a new automated work-order system. They have visualized the data to see how long it takes for a request to be marked as 'in progress' after a student submits it. They are trying to decide if the average response time is a good metric to present to the Dean.
object.prompt: Looking at the visual distribution of request times (where the x-axis is time in hours and the y-axis is the count of requests), you notice a 'long tail' stretching toward the right, meaning many requests are processed quickly, but a few take a very long time. Which description best captures the situation?

options.text: The distribution is perfectly symmetric, so the mean and median are the same.
options.feedback: This ignores the 'long tail' mentioned. A distribution with a long tail is not symmetric.

options.text: The distribution is right-skewed, meaning the mean will be pulled higher than the median by the long-duration requests.
options.feedback: Correct. A right-skewed distribution pulls the mean toward the tail, making the median a more reliable measure of the 'typical' experience.

options.text: The distribution is bimodal, meaning there are two distinct types of work orders being tracked.
options.feedback: The description specifies a 'long tail,' not two separate peaks. Bimodal distributions indicate two clusters, not one cluster with outliers.

options.text: The shape doesn't matter; the mean is always the best way to report the center of any dataset.
options.feedback: The shape is critical; the mean is highly sensitive to extreme values, and using it for skewed data often misrepresents the experience of the majority.

object.hint_content: Consider that a symmetric distribution has a balanced peak, while a skewed distribution has a cluster on one side with a trail on the other. If you report an average (mean), does it accurately reflect the cluster, or is it pulled away by the tail?
object.rubric_primary: The learner identifies that the distribution is skewed and acknowledges that the shape dictates the choice of summary statistic.

visible_thinking_prompts.notice: What is the shape of the data on the graph?
visible_thinking_prompts.think: Why does the 'long tail' exist in a work-order system?
visible_thinking_prompts.wonder: What might happen to our reporting if we only use the mean to describe this distribution?
